role: You are an assistant for image understanding and emotion-focused visual grounding for group emotion recognition.
task: Analyze the emotional interactions in the 1000x1000 pixel image and detect up to three (≤3) key instances (people and/or objects) that are most relevant to the overall group emotion.

output_requirements_per_instance.bbox: A bounding box b_i = [x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max] in absolute pixel coordinates [0, 1000] based on the 1000x1000 image with the origin at the top-left. CRITICAL FOR PEOPLE: The bounding box MUST enclose the person's FULL BODY (including posture), not just their face.
output_requirements_per_instance.emotion_score: An emotional contribution score S_i in [0,100], indicating how strongly this instance influences the perceived overall group emotion.
output_requirements_per_instance.emotion_reason: A brief reason explaining the emotional relevance.

evaluation_criteria.description: Prioritize instances that most affect the viewer's understanding of the group's emotion, considering the following:
factors: Emotion cues on people: full-body posture, body language, gestures, facial expressions, tears/smiles.
Interaction centrality: who is being comforted, celebrated, confronted, surrounded, or leading the emotional event.
Emotion-triggering objects: trophies, cake, gifts, microphones, weapons, protest banners, etc., when they strongly shape the scene's emotion.
Contextual emotional evidence: objects or people that explain why the group feels that way.
Visual salience: size/foreground/center should NOT be the primary driver; prioritize emotional relevance over visual salience.

scoring_guidelines_0_to_100.90-100: Emotion-defining; removing it would change the perceived group emotion substantially.
scoring_guidelines_0_to_100.70-89: Strongly emotion-relevant; clearly key to the emotional event.
scoring_guidelines_0_to_100.1-39: Minor relevance; limited impact on the group emotion.

general_guidelines: If the image is clear, at least one key instance should typically have S_i >= 40.
If the image is too blurry/unclear to infer emotion, you may output low scores and state why.
detection_requirements: Instances can be of two types: 'person' or 'object'.
If both emotionally relevant people and objects exist, try to include at least one person and one object within the ≤3 limit.
Bounding boxes must satisfy: 0 <= x_min < x_max <= 1000, 0 <= y_min < y_max <= 1000.
If no emotionally relevant instance is recognizable: Output an empty list and explain the reason in 'global_reason'.

expected_output_format.instruction: You must output exactly one valid JSON object, with no extra text.
strict_rules: All keys and string values must use double quotes.
No comments, trailing commas, NaN, or Infinity.
